title: ranunculus bongardii var. tenellus
intro: Ranunculus bongardii var. tenellus, also known as Creeping Buttercup, is a perennial flowering plant native to the Kamchatka Peninsula in Russia. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, due to the presence of glycosides and other compounds. The health benefits of Creeping Buttercup include its ability to treat skin conditions, wounds, and infections, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Creeping Buttercup can be prepared as an infusion, tincture, or salve, and is also used in traditional remedies for its antiseptic and antifungal properties.
